name: study-2-spatial-containment
title: Study 2 — spatial containment: holding the individual together
objective:
Study 1 had no space — "inside" was a scalar volume. In a medium the self-produced
interior would diffuse away and the individual would dissolve. Put the cell on a 1-D
lattice where interior content DIFFUSES and the self-produced membrane GATES the
boundary flux, and show that the membrane — built by the metabolism it contains —
is what holds the individual together against dispersion. The autopoiesis of
containment, with precariousness extended to space.

pipeline_gate.proceed_condition:
The interior is held together against diffusion by the self-produced membrane,
and disperses when the metabolism is withdrawn.

behavior_tests.name: containment
behavior_tests.description:
With the self-produced membrane, the interior stays concentrated against diffusion:
the interior/exterior concentration ratio is high (the individual is held together).

provenance.note: 3x interior/exterior concentration is a clear-separation band: the self-produced membrane case (~60x) passes with margin, calibrated so containment is unambiguous rather than marginal. Not a literature value.

behavior_tests.name: membrane-counters-diffusion
behavior_tests.description:
The membrane — not metabolism alone — is what contains: containment with the
membrane is many-fold higher than with metabolism but no membrane.

provenance.note: The membrane must more than double containment vs metabolism-alone for the self-produced boundary (not diffusion physics) to be the cause; 2x is the minimal "membrane dominates" criterion implied by the discriminative claim.

behavior_tests.name: spatial-precariousness
behavior_tests.description:
Containment is held only as a process: starved, the membrane decays, the boundary
leaks, and the individual disperses — retaining only a fraction of its containment.
Measured as starved_containment / held_containment (small = strongly precarious).

deficit_addressed.note: Study 1 had no space — "inside" was a scalar volume that could not disperse. This study adds spatial structure so the self-produced membrane has to hold the individual together against diffusion (no closure-gap store is closed; the deficit is spatial, not a missing self-production step).

findings.statement: The membrane holds the individual together against diffusion: the interior stays 60× more concentrated than the medium with the self-produced membrane, vs 4.4× with metabolism alone. The membrane — not metabolism — is what contains.
Containment is precarious: starved, the membrane decays, the boundary leaks, and the individual disperses — retaining only 4% of its containment. The individual is held together only as a process.

resolved_uncertainties: The self-produced membrane counters diffusion and contains the metabolites; spatial precariousness holds.
remaining_uncertainties: A single deterministic PDE configuration — no parameter sweep over diffusion D / permeability k_perm.
The no-membrane comparison exists in code (membrane=False) but is not recorded as a declared negative control.

followup_study_proposals.title: Containment robustness sweep (D, k_perm)
followup_study_proposals.motivation: Show containment is robust across diffusion/permeability, not a single-configuration artifact (review C4).

followup_study_proposals.title: Record the no-membrane case as a negative control
followup_study_proposals.motivation: The membrane=False run should FAIL containment; declaring it makes the discriminative comparison explicit (review C2/C9).

robustness.note: 5/5 diffusion settings: membrane contains better than no-membrane.

controls.name: self-produced-membrane
controls.hypothesis: A self-produced membrane should contain the metabolites against diffusion.

controls.name: no-membrane
controls.hypothesis: Metabolism WITHOUT a membrane should NOT contain — the interior disperses.

falsifiability: The containment claim would be unsupported if the no-membrane control contained the metabolites as well as the membrane case — i.e. if diffusion physics alone (no self-produced boundary) sufficed.

alternative_hypotheses.claim: The interior stays concentrated because of the geometry / diffusion setup, not the self-produced membrane.
alternative_hypotheses.discriminated_by: the no-membrane negative control disperses (containment ~1x) under the same geometry, isolating the membrane's role.
